asset.alt: HubSpot smart CRM campaign dashboard showing automated marketing workflows

copy.heading: Marketing that runs itself. Almost.
copy.subheading: Breeze automates your campaigns, nurture, and attribution — so the busywork runs on its own and you stay on strategy.

rationale: One hero only, per brief. Chose the hero-product-canvas-panel archetype (drawn as an 'overlay' — one positioning context) so the smart-CRM product graphic IS the canvas (z:back, full-bleed, wide) while a SOLID copy panel (z:front, left inset) carries the tight claim — realizing the sanctioned panel-over-media pair so type never touches the screenshot (no unsanctioned text-on-media, readability holds). It adapts the project hero-photo-overlay pattern: same full-bleed bookend surface and centered-claim intent, but with a solid panel instead of raw text-on-photo because the product screenshot is information-bearing. As the observed opening bookend it takes surfaceIntent:inverse and is the sole accent bearer (the orange period on the display heading — single-accent respected). Copy is tight and non-repeating: eyebrow = register label, heading = the one wry claim in sentence case, subheading advances it with the Breeze automation specifics (campaigns, nurture, attribution). Primary 'Get a demo' + secondary 'See it in action' keep one primary per action group. Real product art (024 customer-platform smart-CRM graphic) is bound — no invented UI.
